name: shopify-automation
description: Automate Shopify tasks via Rube MCP (Composio): products, orders, customers, inventory, collections. Always search tools first for current schemas.
capabilities: List, search, and manage Shopify products with bulk creation support
Retrieve and filter orders by status, payment, and fulfillment state
Access customer data including order history and spending totals
Create and manage product collections with smart rules
Check and update inventory levels across multiple locations
Execute GraphQL queries for advanced Shopify operations
author: sickn33
securitySummary: All 54 static findings are false positives. The skill is documentation for Shopify API operations, not executable code. The backtick patterns flagged as 'Ruby/shell execution' are markdown code formatting for tool names. The hardcoded URL is a legitimate service endpoint required for Rube MCP functionality.
valueStatement: Automate Shopify store management including products, orders, customers, inventory, and collections through natural language commands. Connect via Rube MCP for seamless e-commerce workflow automation.
limitations: Requires active Rube MCP connection with Shopify OAuth authentication
API rate limits apply: 2 requests/second for REST, 1000 points/second for GraphQL
Only supports operations available through Composio Shopify toolkit

useCases.title: E-commerce Store Manager
useCases.description: Quickly add new products in bulk, update inventory across locations, and organize items into collections without logging into Shopify admin.

useCases.title: Order Processing Assistant
useCases.description: Filter and retrieve orders by status, check fulfillment details, and track shipping events for efficient order management.

useCases.title: Customer Data Analyst
useCases.description: Export customer lists with purchase history and spending data for marketing analysis and customer segmentation.

promptTemplates.title: List Recent Orders
promptTemplates.prompt: Show me the last 10 orders with status 'open' from my Shopify store.

promptTemplates.title: Create New Products
promptTemplates.prompt: Create 5 new products with titles 'Summer T-Shirt', 'Winter Jacket', 'Spring Dress', 'Fall Sweater', 'Winter Boots' with vendor 'MyBrand' and status 'active'.

promptTemplates.title: Check Inventory Levels
promptTemplates.prompt: Check inventory levels for inventory items 12345, 12346, and 12347 across all locations.

promptTemplates.title: Build Smart Collection
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a smart collection called 'Sale Items' that automatically includes products with price greater than 50.

bestPractices: Always verify Rube MCP connection status is ACTIVE before running workflows
Use pagination with cursor-based navigation for large product catalogs
Specify explicit status filters when retrieving orders to avoid missing results
antiPatterns: Assuming connection is active without checking status first
Processing large datasets without implementing pagination logic
Ignoring rate limits and receiving API throttling errors

faq.question: Do I need a Shopify API key?
faq.answer: No API keys required. The skill uses Rube MCP which handles OAuth authentication with your Shopify store through the browser.

faq.question: What Shopify operations are supported?
faq.answer: Products, orders, customers, inventory, collections, fulfillments, and GraphQL queries are all supported through the Composio toolkit.

faq.question: How do I handle large product catalogs?
faq.answer: Use paginated endpoints with cursor-based pagination. Check for 'next' link in responses and continue until all pages are processed.

faq.question: Can I create products in bulk?
faq.answer: Yes, use SHOPIFY_BULK_CREATE_PRODUCTS to create multiple products in a single API call.

faq.question: What rate limits should I be aware of?
faq.answer: REST API allows 2 requests per second on standard plans. GraphQL allows 1000 cost points per second. Implement delays for bulk operations.

faq.question: How do I verify my connection is working?
faq.answer: Call SHOPIFY_VALIDATE_ACCESS to confirm your store connection is active and properly authenticated.
